panels.description: Network throughput on Kubernetes monitoring/loadtest nodes.

options.content:
### Recommended source of truth

- For PostgreSQL host CPU, RAM, disk latency, throughput, and network, use DSM / VMware VCF / vSphere provider-side metrics for the DB VM or service.
- The panels above only cover the Kubernetes monitoring and load-test cluster.
- If provider metrics are exported into Prometheus later, map them to this section.

### Target metrics

- CPU utilization %
- Memory utilization %
- Disk busy %, read/write throughput, latency
- Network throughput

panels.description: Final TPS pushed by pgbench jobs after each run.
panels.title: Last Run TPS

panels.description: Average latency from the last completed pgbench run.
panels.title: Latency Avg

panels.description: Latency standard deviation from the last completed pgbench run.
panels.title: Latency Stddev

panels.description: P95 latency computed from pgbench transaction logs.
panels.title: Latency P95

panels.description: P99 latency computed from pgbench transaction logs.
panels.title: Latency P99

panels.description: Failed transaction count from the last completed pgbench run.
panels.title: Error Count

panels.description: Transactions successfully processed in the last completed pgbench run.
panels.title: Processed Tx

panels.description: Whether the last pgbench run completed successfully.
panels.title: Run Success

panels.description: Step-style history of final TPS values pushed by pgbench runs.
panels.title: pgbench TPS History

panels.description: History of average and tail latency from completed pgbench runs.
panels.title: pgbench Latency History

options.content:
### Current coverage

- PostgreSQL metrics in this dashboard come from `postgres_exporter`.
- Endpoint availability comes from `blackbox_exporter`.
- `pgbench` run output now comes from job metrics pushed through `Pushgateway`.
- `pg_auto_failover` is event-driven. Use the companion push script during failover drills to record state, RTO, RPO, and replay lag into Pushgateway.

### Important note

The OS panels currently reflect nodes exposed by `node_exporter`. For DSM database VM-level CPU/RAM/IO metrics, keep using provider-side metrics if DB hosts are not directly instrumented.

panels.title: [Manual] Failover RTO

panels.description: Optional manual RPO captured during failover drill.
panels.title: [Manual] Failover RPO

panels.description: Optional manual timeline for pg_auto_failover state changes during HA testing.
panels.title: [Manual] Failover State Timeline

panels.description: Optional replay lag captured at failover event time.
panels.title: [Manual] Event Replay Lag
